questions.q: In which city does the Brihadisvara temple stand?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara temple was built at Thanjavur, the Chola capital.

questions.q: Which Chola emperor built the Brihadisvara temple?
questions.explanation: The temple was commissioned by Rajaraja Chola I.

questions.q: To which deity is the Brihadisvara temple dedicated?
questions.explanation: The temple is a great Shiva temple, with a colossal lingam in its sanctum.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple is a fine example of which architectural style?
questions.explanation: It is a masterpiece of Dravidian temple architecture as developed by the Cholas.

questions.q: Around which year was the Brihadisvara temple completed?
questions.explanation: The temple was completed around 1010 CE during Rajaraja I's reign.

questions.q: The towering pyramidal structure over the sanctum of the temple is called what?
questions.explanation: The tower rising over the main sanctum is the vimana, the temple's most famous feature.

questions.q: The Brihadisvara temple holds what international status?
questions.explanation: The temple is inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site among the Great Living Chola Temples.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Brihadisvara temple located?
questions.explanation: Thanjavur and its great temple lie in the state of Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: A huge monolithic statue of which mount of Shiva guards the temple?
questions.explanation: A massive monolithic Nandi (Shiva's bull mount) faces the sanctum.

questions.q: The temple is one of a group of UNESCO-listed monuments collectively known as what?
questions.explanation: It is part of the 'Great Living Chola Temples' World Heritage listing.

questions.q: What is the original name given to the temple by Rajaraja I himself?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja named the temple Rajarajeshwaram after himself; 'Brihadisvara' is a later Sanskritised name.

questions.q: Approximately how tall is the temple's main vimana?
questions.explanation: The vimana rises to roughly 66 metres (about 216 feet), making it one of the tallest of its era.

questions.q: The crowning stone (shikhara/cupola) atop the vimana is a single block weighing roughly how much?
questions.explanation: The massive capstone is estimated to weigh around 80 tonnes, a feat of engineering.

questions.q: Of what primary material is the Brihadisvara temple chiefly built?
questions.explanation: The temple is constructed largely of granite, unusual given its scale and the local geology.

questions.q: The detailed inscriptions covering the temple's plinth chiefly record what information?
questions.explanation: The base inscriptions list extensive gifts of gold, land and the many servants attached to the temple.

questions.q: The temple's sanctum houses one of the largest examples of which sacred object?
questions.explanation: The garbhagriha enshrines a very large lingam, the aniconic form of Shiva.

questions.q: Fresco-style wall paintings of which period were discovered beneath later layers in the temple?
questions.explanation: Original Chola-era frescoes were found beneath later Nayaka paintings within the temple corridors.

questions.q: Who is traditionally named as the chief architect of the Brihadisvara temple?
questions.explanation: Inscriptions name Kunjara Mallan Raja Raja Perunthachan as the principal architect.

questions.q: The temple complex is entered through grand gateway towers known as what?
questions.explanation: Visitors pass through monumental gopurams (gateway towers) to reach the temple courtyard.

questions.q: The 2010 celebrations at the temple marked which anniversary of its completion?
questions.explanation: In 2010 grand festivities celebrated the temple's 1000th anniversary.

questions.q: Carvings on the temple walls depict numerous postures of which classical art form?
questions.explanation: Sculpted panels illustrate the karanas, the basic dance poses of Bharatanatyam.

questions.q: Which dynasty, ruling Thanjavur after the Cholas, added shrines and paintings to the temple complex?
questions.explanation: The Thanjavur Nayaks and later the Marathas made additions to the temple over the centuries.

questions.q: A persistent popular claim about the vimana's capstone (that its shadow never falls on the ground at noon) is best described as what?
options: A popular myth not supported by evidence
questions.explanation: The 'no shadow at noon' story is a widely repeated legend; the tower does cast a shadow and the claim is not factual.

questions.q: The temple's Nandi was carved from a single stone roughly how long?
questions.explanation: The monolithic Nandi is roughly 6 metres (about 19 feet) long, one of the largest in India.

questions.q: An inner wall of the temple bears a rare early depiction of which dance form by Shiva, accompanied by detailed sculpture?
questions.explanation: The temple includes notable depictions of Shiva as the dancing Nataraja and the karana dance poses.

questions.q: How is the enormous capstone believed to have been raised to the top of the vimana?
options: Carried by trained elephants up stairs
Hauled up a long inclined ramp
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that a great inclined earthen ramp was used to drag the capstone to the summit.

questions.q: The temple was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage list in which year?
questions.explanation: The Brihadisvara temple was added to the World Heritage list in 1987.

questions.q: Bronze images of which Chola-era saints and deities were donated to and housed in the temple, reflecting Chola metalwork?
options: Shaiva saints and forms of Shiva
questions.explanation: The temple received numerous bronze images of Shaiva saints and forms of Shiva, hallmarks of Chola bronze art.

questions.q: The temple's sister monument, Gangaikondacholisvaram, was built by whom in emulation of it?
questions.explanation: Rajaraja's son Rajendra I built the closely related temple at his new capital Gangaikonda Cholapuram.

questions.q: The outer prakara (enclosure) wall of the temple is notable for housing what feature along its inner side?
options: A cloister with rows of lingams and a pillared corridor
questions.explanation: The enclosing wall has a long pillared cloister lined with many lingams encircling the courtyard.
